pullRequest.title: feat: add boto3 client support
pullRequest.body:
### **User description** ## Description This PR enhances the `BedrockEncoder` class to support passing a pre-configured boto3 client as an alternative to providing individual AWS credentials. ### Changes Made **Core Enhancement:** - Added optional `client` parameter to `BedrockEncoder.__init__()` - Modified initialization logic to use provided boto3 client when available - Falls back to credential-based initialization when no client is provided **Code Changes:** - **New Parameter**: Added `client: Optional[Any] = None` parameter - **Conditional Logic**: Implemented if/else logic to handle both initialization methods: - If `client` is provided: Use the pre-configured client directly - If `client` is None: Use existing credential-based initialization flow - **Backward Compatibility**: Maintains full backward compatibility with existing code ### Benefits 1. **Flexibility**: Users can now pass pre-configured boto3 clients for better control 2. **Integration**: Easier integration with existing applications already using boto3 3. **Configuration**: More granular control over client configuration (retries, timeouts, etc.) 4. **Backward Compatible**: Existing code continues to work without changes ### Usage Examples **New Method (with boto3 client):** ```python import boto3 from semantic_router.encoders import BedrockEncoder boto3_client = boto3.client("bedrock-runtime", region_name="us-west-1") encoder = BedrockEncoder(name="amazon.titan-embed-text-v1", client=boto3_client) ``` **Existing Method (still supported):** ```python encoder = BedrockEncoder( name="amazon.titan-embed-text-v1", access_key_id="...", secret_access_key="...", region="us-west-1" ) ``` ### Documentation - Updated notebook documentation to explain the alternative boto3 client approach - Added clear explanation of when and why to use each method ___ ### **PR Type** Enhancement, Documentation ___ ### **Description** - Add optional `client` to `BedrockEncoder` - Use provided boto3 client when available - Fallback to env/credentials-based initialization - Add notebook documenting both initialization methods ___ ### Diagram Walkthrough ```mermaid flowchart LR Init["BedrockEncoder.__init__ adds optional client"] UseClient["Use passed boto3 client"] Fallback["Fallback: build client from creds/env"] Docs["Add notebook: credential and client examples"] Init -- "client provided" --> UseClient Init -- "no client" --> Fallback Init -- "document usage" --> Docs ```

File Walkthrough
Relevant files
Enhancement
bedrock.py
Support injected boto3 client in BedrockEncoder                   
semantic_router/encoders/bedrock.py
  • Add client: Optional[Any] init parameter
  • If client provided, assign directly
  • Else, read AWS creds/region and initialize client
  • Preserve error handling on client initialization
 +21/-17 
Documentation
bedrock.ipynb
Notebook documenting BedrockEncoder initialization options
bedrock.ipynb
  • Add end-to-end Bedrock usage notebook
  • Show credentials-based initialization
  • Show pre-configured boto3 client injection
  • Include example routing workflow and outputs
 +1344/-0
___

pullRequest.title: fix: add limit to acall and fix async hybrid router
pullRequest.body:
This PR will resolve https://github.com/aurelio-labs/semantic-router/issues/586, https://github.com/aurelio-labs/semantic-router/issues/593, and https://github.com/aurelio-labs/semantic-router/issues/589

This pull request enhances the functionality of the `semantic_router` module by introducing asynchronous methods, improving error handling, and adding test coverage for new and existing features. The most significant changes include the addition of an asynchronous `acall` method to the `HybridRouter` class, support for a `limit` parameter in routing methods, and improved test cases to validate these updates.

### Enhancements to Router Functionality:

* **Asynchronous Routing Method**: Added an `acall` method to the `HybridRouter` class, enabling asynchronous routing with support for both dense and sparse vectors. This includes validation for required inputs and integration with the async index query mechanism. (`semantic_router/routers/hybrid.py`, [semantic_router/routers/hybrid.pyR363-R426](diffhunk://#diff-8d989ab449cf069a0832e0c961584e204ecd6af0b1f2b4ae4f82b9cfbb01dffdR363-R426))
* **Support for `limit` Parameter**: Updated the `acall` method in `semantic_router/routers/base.py` to accept a `limit` parameter, allowing users to control the number of route choices returned. The `limit` parameter is also passed to `_async_pass_routes` for further processing. (`semantic_router/routers/base.py`, [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-5469c6c5b738ad6af8b68ff6868e721a3c5577af16c299ece5241354e7e41035R762) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-5469c6c5b738ad6af8b68ff6868e721a3c5577af16c299ece5241354e7e41035L799-R802)

### Error Handling Improvements:

* **Enhanced Error Messages**: Improved error messages in the `__call__` method of `HybridRouter` to provide more descriptive feedback when required components (e.g., sparse encoder) are not set. (`semantic_router/routers/hybrid.py`, [semantic_router/routers/hybrid.pyL322-R322](diffhunk://#diff-8d989ab449cf069a0832e0c961584e204ecd6af0b1f2b4ae4f82b9cfbb01dffdL322-R322))

### Test Suite Updates:

* **New Test Cases for `limit` Parameter**: Added unit tests to validate the behavior of the `limit` parameter in both synchronous and asynchronous router calls. These tests ensure correct handling of different limit values, including `None` (return all matches). (`tests/unit/test_router.py`, [tests/unit/test_router.pyR656-R840](diffhunk://#diff-68d2964608cb9c8cbb97f888938b8aaf50062b04354365240181151a24608d0bR656-R840))
* **Fixture and Imports**: Introduced a new `routes_5` fixture and updated imports to include `RouteChoice`, ensuring proper setup for the new test cases. (`tests/unit/test_router.py`, [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-68d2964608cb9c8cbb97f888938b8aaf50062b04354365240181151a24608d0bL27-R27) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-68d2964608cb9c8cbb97f888938b8aaf50062b04354365240181151a24608d0bR209-R218)

pullRequest.title: feat: postgres async functions added
pullRequest.body:
This pull request introduces several updates to the `semantic_router` module, focusing on improving asynchronous functionality, updating dependencies, and refining initialization processes. Key changes include the addition of asynchronous methods, updates to Pinecone API versioning, and modifications to class constructors for better data handling.

### Dependency Updates:
* Updated the `pinecone` library dependency in `pyproject.toml` to version `>=6.0.0`.
* Changed the Pinecone API version from `"2024-07"` to `"2025-04"` in `semantic_router/index/pinecone.py`.

### Asynchronous Enhancements:
* Added `ais_ready` methods to check initialization readiness asynchronously across multiple files (`semantic_router/index/base.py`, `semantic_router/index/local.py`, `semantic_router/index/pinecone.py`). [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-df154312e842c3302d871397cc51942d0a6e3be19183c0b362499fb8e2fcd127R225-R233) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-3f7c4d16e4e8638997135efd56b4c4423ca8bd5f111901351d37177ca40dfe98R119-R126) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-c57f54339773ad317ee6eafd071395202933f9eeaf59a10c28fc22d06bf564a2L1101-R1113)
* Introduced asynchronous versions of key methods such as `alen`, `aadd`, `adelete`, `aquery`, and `_async_get_all` in `semantic_router/index/pinecone.py` to support async operations. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-c57f54339773ad317ee6eafd071395202933f9eeaf59a10c28fc22d06bf564a2R1417-R1452) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-ff36ad62064e1d63dcbae87464846e5a62ba551a5c13b1efe57313edc7ac263eR74-R109) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-c57f54339773ad317ee6eafd071395202933f9eeaf59a10c28fc22d06bf564a2R742-L736) [[4]](diffhunk://#diff-c57f54339773ad317ee6eafd071395202933f9eeaf59a10c28fc22d06bf564a2L975-R986) [[5]](diffhunk://#diff-c57f54339773ad317ee6eafd071395202933f9eeaf59a10c28fc22d06bf564a2L1254-R1285)

### Code Refinements:
* Modified the constructors in `HybridLocalIndex` and `LocalIndex` to accept keyword arguments (`**data`) for better flexibility. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-ff36ad62064e1d63dcbae87464846e5a62ba551a5c13b1efe57313edc7ac263eL16-R17) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-3f7c4d16e4e8638997135efd56b4c4423ca8bd5f111901351d37177ca40dfe98L15-R16)
* Renamed `async_init_index` to `init_async_index` in `BaseRouter` to improve clarity and consistency. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-5469c6c5b738ad6af8b68ff6868e721a3c5577af16c299ece5241354e7e41035L363-R363) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-5469c6c5b738ad6af8b68ff6868e721a3c5577af16c299ece5241354e7e41035L377-R377) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-5469c6c5b738ad6af8b68ff6868e721a3c5577af16c299ece5241354e7e41035L439-R439)

### Pinecone Index Improvements:
* Enhanced the `_init_async_index` method in `semantic_router/index/pinecone.py` to calculate and manage the `index_host` attribute more effectively. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-c57f54339773ad317ee6eafd071395202933f9eeaf59a10c28fc22d06bf564a2L419-R421) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c57f54339773ad317ee6eafd071395202933f9eeaf59a10c28fc22d06bf564a2L445-R463)
* Improved error handling in asynchronous methods to raise exceptions when the index is not ready or initialized. [[1]](diffhunk://#diff-c57f54339773ad317ee6eafd071395202933f9eeaf59a10c28fc22d06bf564a2R742-L736) [[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c57f54339773ad317ee6eafd071395202933f9eeaf59a10c28fc22d06bf564a2L975-R986) [[3]](diffhunk://#diff-c57f54339773ad317ee6eafd071395202933f9eeaf59a10c28fc22d06bf564a2L1254-R1285)

pullRequest.title: chore: update openai sdk to v2.x
pullRequest.body:
Updates OpenAI SDK dependency to support v2.x (>=2.8.0).

  Closes #647

  ## Changes
  - Updated `openai` dependency from `>=1.10.0,<2.0.0` to `>=2.8.0`

  ## Testing
  All 69 OpenAI-related tests pass:
  - ✅ OpenAI encoder tests (13/13)
  - ✅ Azure OpenAI encoder tests (13/13)
  - ✅ OpenAI LLM tests (23/23)
  - ✅ Azure OpenAI LLM tests (8/8)
  - ✅ OpenRouter LLM tests (6/6)
  - ✅ LiteLLM OpenAI tests (6/6)

  ## Notes
  No code changes required - semantic-router already uses the modern OpenAI client-based API which is fully compatible with v2.x.
